claims.evidence: game.js init(): state.state='playing', score=0, player=SI.Player.create(gameWidth,gameHeight) (centered x=(w-40)/2), aliens=SI.Alien.createGrid() (5x11 via Config.ALIEN_ROWS/COLS, pointsForRow gives 30/20/20/10/10 = HIGH/MID/MID/LOW/LOW), playerBullets=[], alienBullets=[]; getOrCreateState() reuses existing window.gameState object and init() reassigns every contract field, so re-init after mutation is a clean slate.
player.js updatePlayer(): after applying SPEED to x, clamps `if (player.x < 0) player.x = 0` and `maxX = gameWidth - player.width; if (player.x > maxX) player.x = maxX`.

game.js update(): `var fireEdge = input.fire && !prevFire; prevFire = input.fire; if (fireEdge) playerBullets.push(...)` — single push only on false->true transition, holding fire keeps prevFire true so no repeat spawns.

game.js resolveBulletAlienCollisions() calls window.SI.Collision.aabbOverlap(toAabb(bullet), toAabb(alien)); on hit, alien is dropped from survivingAliens and `state.score += alien.points` (exact row value); Bullet.spawnPlayerBullet returns a bare {x,y,width,height} object with no velocity/alive field, and array membership alone makes it collidable (bullet.js comment confirms).

game.js update(dt) is called once per fixed step by loop.js's accumulator (`while (accumulator >= step) update(step)`); player.js SPEED=5 and bullet.js SPEED=8 are fixed per-step constants never multiplied by the dt argument, so movement magnitude is dt-independent.

game.js: `window.gameState = state; window.SI.Game.state = state;` same object reference; getOrCreateState() reuses window.gameState across calls instead of allocating a new object, so identity holds after bootstrap and across re-init.

claims.text: gameState carries extra fields beyond the slice contract (lives, wave, fps, shields, ufo stub) already wired for later slices.
SI.RNG is a seedable mulberry32 PRNG with deterministic default seed(1), and init(opts.seed) can reseed it — not required by any c1-c6 claim.
loop.js implements a fixed-timestep accumulator with a spiral-of-death guard (caps catch-up delta at 3x step) — robustness beyond what c5 strictly requires.
alien objects retain an `alive: true` and `row` field even though collision resolution removes dead aliens via array filtering rather than reading `alive`.
No MAX_PLAYER_BULLETS or similar cap exists on playerBullets array size; only off-screen (y+height<=0) bullets are pruned per step.
